text.content:
Scene descriptions are included in-line with the scenes as they appear, the first time they're used in the game. Some of those are duplicated here; others (environments, mostly) have extra details for describing the scene, which gangs are usually present, etc.

To access these descriptions in-game, right click the scene in the top ribbon or in the scenes menu, and select "scene notes" from the dropdown.

66 Lincoln Blvd. isn’t much to look at from the outside. The Upper Marina was the first district in Night City to be rebuilt after the bombing of Arasaka Tower, and you’re surrounded on all sides by the glitz and glitter of Downtown Night City. The homeless still lurk in alleyways, and the local gang presence is still watching the streets, but there’s no gunfire and NCPD armored patrol cars roll by regularly, protecting the new corporate interests, there.

The front of the building you've come to see is understated and nondescript. On its left side is the entrance to an underground parking garage with its shutter closed. Beyond that is a cybernetics sales association, its front windows filled with reruns of bloody, cyber-augmented UFC matches from the last year. On the right is a high-end counseling center, its storefront and sidewalk lined with holographic emitters of beautiful, well-dressed counselors promising comfort and happiness in sincere, angelic voices. 

By comparison, your own address is so quiet that a passerby wouldn’t even notice it. A stylized, neon-red A glares with light above a pair of double doors. The building is 9 stories tall, abutting the sidewalk, and adorned by red LED strips to cast a bloody light over the street. It’s quiet, and the entrance is deserted. The windows, both on the ground level and upper floors, are all tinted, and look like they’ve been recently cleaned.

In place of door handles on the double doors, there are a pair of coin slots and a complex looking locking apparatus next to them. A door-cam strip is roughly at eye level above each mechanism. No one, and nothing else, is present save the party - the street deserted except for the occasional car or pedestrian, hurrying about in the miserable, persistent drizzle.

Inside the double doors, a three-story-high hotel lobby greets you, lined with spacious seating arrangements of furniture on either side of the main walkway, all of which is covered by plastic sheets as though not yet ready for use. These side areas are partitioned by panels of reinforced, bulletproof wood and frosted bulletproof glass. At the front of the room, about 30 feet ahead, there are exits to other parts of the building on the left and right sides and the pathway inwards stops in front of an empty concierge's desk. Behind it are a pair of elevators, with a stair access between them.

A simulated dusk is the room’s primary source of light, shining a golden glow from above the elevators. Holographic projectors are managing an expanse of fluffy clouds beneath blue skies. Unless you frequent the Deep Badlands, this is likely the first time you’ve seen a sky this blue in 20 years - the coastal skies are either some light tone of gray or red, most of the time.

South Night City - "the bad part of town." The area hit the worst by CW4, and the last area that hasn't been rebuilt. Dominated by the homeless, gangs, and people too desperate to be able to live anywhere else. In short - an edgerunner's paradise.

The streets are pockmarked with craters like a Lace addict's face, and at the district's edges, are being washed into the water. The sturdier buildings are riddled with holes and covered in gang tagging - the less sturdy ones are all in some form of collapse. Rusted out vehicles line the streets, couriers scurry around on skateboards and bicycles, homeless junkies crowd the alleyways, gangs patrol the streets, and the bloody red sunsets make the muddy streets run with blood.

The gunfire that can be heard across half of Night City is closer, here.

Gang Presence

  • Scavs - major

  • 6th Street - major

  • All other gangs - minor to moderate

Past the sprawl of rampant urbanism, once the streets have lost their sidewalks, lies the sprawl of Night City's burgeoning industry. Sure, Zhirafa owns the manufacturing plants in Heywood, but what about the CHOOH and steel refineries, the chemical factories, mass storage, and all the other dirty industry that keeps an isolated city of millions fed and humming?

This close to the edge of the city, the wastes have started to take over. Everything is coated in a thick layer of desert sand and dust, and the sky is perpetually tinted grey or yellow from the reflections and the pollution. The claustrophobia of the inner city's towering apartments and cloistered streets is quickly replaced by miles of piping and dull, low warehouses. The smell of city sewers, still water, low tide and garbage fade with the change, replaced by the noxious smell of unregulated chemical emissions that burns your nose on the way in and your tongue on the way out. Automated corporate security is everywhere, and the gang tagging has faded somewhat as a result.

Now all you've got to worry about is one of the autoguns malfunctioning.

Gang Presence

  • Corporations (all - RatSec in general), Rackoners, Scavs - major

  • 6th Street, Maelstrom, Scavs, Piranhas - minor

Faded brownstones line cobblestone sidewalks and well-paved streets, which are themselves clogged with corporate taxis and combat cabs, all of it crammed with the hustle and bustle of what scarcely remains of Night City's middle class. Here, you can almost forget about the Red Decades. Until you look down an alley and see the homeless camps that were pushed to the sides by the NCPD, or get up above the street noise and hear the gunshots from the combat zones, or the wind blows the wrong way from the old city center and the city PA system reminds you to take an iodine pill.

Despite the NCPD presence, it's still anything but safe. Corporate interests line the sidewalks, execs carry out deals in the shadows, and amid the crowd, if someone were to vanish, nobody would even notice.

Still, at least here you don't feel like you have to be strapped just to hit the bodega.

Gang Presence

  • NCPD, corps (all - RatSec in general) - major

  • Philharmonic Vampyres, Piranhas, Prime-Time Players - Minor
